user_scenarios.user_goal: As a crawler workbench operator, configure channel credentials, verify access, and see clear channel readiness decisions without exposing plaintext secrets.
prerequisites: Crawler workbench backend dependencies are installed.
The test uses synthetic secrets and local HTTP fixtures only.
No real token, cookie, or private credential is required.
steps: Create an isolated state directory and initialize channel tables.
Create channels covering public, auth-required, browser-like, network-failed, and unsupported cases.
Write and replace a synthetic token secret through the API.
Read the channel and confirm the secret is represented only as configured metadata.
Run probe requests against deterministic local HTTP fixtures.
List probe runs and inspect persisted latest channel readiness fields.
Attempt a source run against disabled, auth-failed, and needs-browser channels.
expected_outcomes: A secrets.key file is created under the isolated state directory with restrictive permissions when supported.
Secret plaintext is never returned by channel list, detail, or probe APIs.
Probe history records ready, needs_auth_config, auth_failed, needs_browser, network_failed, and unsupported outcomes.
Probe summaries do not include secret values, authorization headers, cookies, or raw response bodies.
Missing or unreadable key material fails closed when encrypted secrets exist.
Source runs blocked by channel readiness write clear fetch_runs.error messages.
failure_signals: Synthetic secret text appears in API responses, probe summaries, logs, or persisted probe history.
Encrypted secrets can be silently ignored when the key file is missing.
Probe outcomes do not update channel latest status fields.
A disabled, auth-failed, or needs-browser channel still allows a source run.
cleanup: Remove temporary state directories and local HTTP fixture processes created by pytest.
